name: urivision runtime catalog
summary: Serve urivision's CapabilityRegistry — the URI-addressed contract map of the perception/action stack (ui://, input://, view://, guard://…) — as runtime:// routes: catalog, explain, and a guarded dry-run-by-default dispatch.
description: urivision's runtime is a deliberately mock-backed CapabilityRegistry: stable URI contracts with schemas, preconditions and safety tags, while the real execution lives in urirun connectors (kvm/vql/vdisplay). This connector gives the mesh the missing piece — the CATALOG — so a flow, planner, or node can discover and reason over that contract map without duplicating any implementation. capabilities/query/list returns the full catalog (json, markdown, prompt or tools format); capability/query/explain resolves one URI to its capability, captures and expected payload; capability/command/call dispatches through the registry, dry-run by default (validate + show what WOULD run), execute=true to invoke the mock-backed handler.
useCases: Give an LLM planner the full capability catalog (prompt/tools format) as its action space.
Resolve a URI to its contract — schema, preconditions, safety tag — before executing it elsewhere.
Validate a payload against a capability (dry-run call) as a cheap preflight gate.
